source: Exhibit A — Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements, McDonald's USA, LLC, audited by Ernst & Young LLP (clean opinion March 14 2025)

nature_of_business.value: McDonald's USA, LLC franchises and operates McDonald's restaurants in the U.S.; restaurants are operated either by the Company or by franchisees (conventional franchisees and affiliates under license agreements). Wholly-owned subsidiary of McDonald's Corporation; on January 1, 2005, McDonald's Corporation contributed assets net of liabilities of $5,548.8 million to the Company.

restaurant_counts_per_note_1.note: Note 1 totals differ slightly from Item 20 Table 1 (which reports 12,887 franchised + 672 company-owned = 13,559 at end of 2024) — 1-restaurant gap on franchised, 1-restaurant gap on company-owned, 2-restaurant gap on system total.

consolidation.policy: Consolidated financial statements include the Company and its subsidiaries. The Company evaluates business relationships such as those with franchisees, joint venture partners, suppliers and advertising cooperatives to identify potential variable interest entities. Generally, these qualify for a scope exception under VIE consolidation guidance. The Company has concluded consolidation of any such entity is not appropriate for the periods presented.
consolidation.VIE_election: VIEs (franchisees, JV partners, suppliers, advertising co-ops) NOT consolidated; concluded the Company is not the primary beneficiary and lacks the power through voting or similar rights to direct activities of the cooperatives that most significantly impact their economic performance.

estimates.policy: Preparation in conformity with U.S. GAAP requires management estimates and assumptions; actual results could differ.

recent_accounting_pronouncements_not_yet.std: ASU 2023-09 (Income Taxes Topic 740) — expanded rate-reconciliation and income-taxes-paid disclosures
ASU 2024-03 (Income Statement / Subtopic 220-40) — disaggregation of income statement expenses
recent_accounting_pronouncements_not_yet.effective: fiscal years beginning after Dec 15 2026; interim periods after Dec 15 2027

revenue_recognition.company_owned_sales: Recognized on a cash basis at the time of the underlying sale, net of sales tax and other sales-related taxes.
revenue_recognition.royalty_revenue: Based on a percent of sales; recognized at the time the underlying sales occur.
revenue_recognition.rental_income: Includes both minimum rent payments (recognized straight-line over the franchise term) and variable rent payments based on a percent of sales (recognized at the time the underlying sales occur).
revenue_recognition.initial_fees: Recognized as the Company satisfies the performance obligation over the franchise term (generally 20 years).
revenue_recognition.technology_platform_revenue: Goods/services related to various technology platforms are distinct from the franchise agreement; the Company is the principal; revenue presented gross on the income statement; recognized as goods/services are transferred to the franchisee; related expenses recorded primarily in Other restaurant expenses.
revenue_recognition.brand_licensing_arrangement_revenue: Based on a percent of sales; recognized at the time the underlying sales occur.

useful_lives.leasehold_improvements: lesser of useful life of asset or lease term (which generally include certain option periods)

property_and_equipment.intercompany_legal_title: McDonald's Corporation has retained legal title to certain property and equipment assets used by the Company under extended intercompany agreements; such assets are included in the property and equipment balances.
property_and_equipment.shared_costs_with_franchisees: Where the Company and a franchisee share project costs, the Company manages the project, provides upfront funding, and allocates costs between Property and equipment and Accounts receivable; final allocation may result in immaterial adjustments.

leasing_lessee_policy.lessee_portfolio: Significant real estate portfolio, primarily ground leases (Company leases land, generally owns building) and improved leases (Company leases land and buildings).
leasing_lessee_policy.discount_rate_basis: Rate implicit in the lease if determinable, otherwise collateralized incremental borrowing rate considering term and currency environment.
leasing_lessee_policy.short_term_lease_election: Leases with initial term of 12 months or less (primarily office equipment) are not on balance sheet; recognized straight-line.
leasing_lessee_policy.non_lease_components: Company has elected NOT to separate non-lease components from lease components in its lessee portfolio; impact of including occupancy costs is immaterial and generally limited to company-owned restaurant locations.
leasing_lessee_policy.franchised_occupancy_pass_through: For franchised locations (the majority of the portfolio), occupancy costs (property taxes, insurance, site maintenance) are generally required to be paid by franchisees as part of the franchise arrangement.

capitalized_software.policy: Stated at cost; amortized straight-line over estimated useful life, primarily 3 to 10 years; customer-facing software typically amortized over a shorter useful life; back office and corporate systems may have a longer useful life.
capitalized_software.impairment_review: Reviewed whenever events or changes in circumstances indicate the carrying amount may not be recoverable; no indicators of material impairment identified for 2024, 2023 or 2022.

long_lived_assets_impairment_policy.annual_review_timing: Fourth quarter, plus whenever indicators exist
long_lived_assets_impairment_policy.asset_grouping_for_impairment: Restaurants in the U.S. are initially grouped at the field office level (not individual restaurant) — the Company manages restaurants as a portfolio with significant common costs and promotional activities and concludes individual restaurant cash flows are not generally independent of others in a market.
long_lived_assets_impairment_policy.test_methodology: If indicator of impairment exists for a grouping, undiscounted future cash flows by individual restaurant compared to its carrying value; impaired restaurants measured at excess of carrying amount over fair value (estimate of discounted future cash flows).
long_lived_assets_impairment_policy.held_for_disposal: Losses recognized when management/Board (as required) approve and commit to a plan, assets are available for disposal, disposal is probable within 12 months, and net sales proceeds are expected to be less than net book value.

goodwill_policy.source: Excess of cost over net tangible and identifiable intangible assets of acquired restaurants and other businesses; primarily from purchases of McDonald's restaurants from franchisees.

2024_acquisitions.purpose: expand company-owned and operated footprint in key growth areas and to support key strategic franchising initiatives

goodwill_policy.writeoff_on_resale_within_24_months: If a Company-owned restaurant is sold within 24 months of acquisition, goodwill is written off in its entirety; if sold beyond 24 months, write-off is based on relative fair value of business sold compared to fair value of reporting unit.
goodwill_policy.annual_test_timing: Fourth quarter; current year qualitative assessment found no indicators of impairment; historically goodwill impairment has not significantly impacted the consolidated financial statements.

advertising_costs.franchisee_contributions_treatment: Significant advertising costs are also incurred by conventional franchisees through contributions to advertising cooperatives; McDonald's is not the primary beneficiary of these entities and does not consolidate them.

income_taxes_policy.provision_basis: Separate-return basis; settlements made through intercompany accounts with McDonald's Corporation
income_taxes_policy.filing: U.S. federal earnings included in McDonald's consolidated U.S. federal income tax return; state filings may be combined or separate depending on state law
income_taxes_policy.uncertain_tax_positions_methodology: More-likely-than-not threshold for recognition; interest and penalties on unrecognized tax benefits recorded in provision for income taxes

property_and_equipment_detail_in_notes.intercompany_legal_title_note: McDonald's Corporation has retained legal title to certain property and equipment assets used by the Company under extended intercompany agreements; such assets are included in the amounts above.

franchise_arrangements_in_notes.structure: Conventional franchise arrangements include a lease and a license; payment of initial fees plus continuing rent and royalties based on percent of sales with minimum rent payments; minimum rent based on the Company's underlying investment in owned sites and parallel underlying leases and escalations on properties leased.
franchise_arrangements_in_notes.post_term: At end of 20 years, the Company maintains control of the underlying real estate and building and can either enter into a new 20-year franchise arrangement with the existing franchisee or a different franchisee, or close the restaurant.
franchise_arrangements_in_notes.occupancy_costs: Franchisees generally pay related occupancy costs including property taxes, insurance and site maintenance.
franchise_arrangements_in_notes.consideration_allocation_election: The Company has elected to allocate consideration in the franchise contract among lease and non-lease components in the same manner historically: rental income (lease), royalty income (non-lease) and initial fee income (non-lease).

leasing_arrangements_in_notes.lease_term_typical: Generally 20 years for restaurants where market conditions allow; with rent escalations and renewal options
leasing_arrangements_in_notes.renewal_options: Typically solely at the Company's discretion

contingencies_note.language: In the ordinary course of business, the Company is subject to proceedings, lawsuits and other claims primarily related to competitors, customers, employees, franchisees, government agencies, intellectual property, shareholders and suppliers. The Company does not believe that any such matter currently being reviewed will have a material adverse effect on its financial condition or results of operations.

restructuring_initiative_accelerating_th.purpose: Modernize ways of working; faster, more innovative, more efficient
restructuring_initiative_accelerating_th.current_year_charge_composition: Primarily employee termination benefits; no significant non-cash impairment charges
restructuring_initiative_accelerating_th.expected_payout: Substantially all of the accrued balance at Dec 31 2024 expected to be paid out over the next twelve months.

income_taxes_note.tax_examination_periods: With few exceptions, the Company is no longer subject to U.S. federal, state, and local income tax examinations by tax authorities for years before 2009.

employee_benefit_plans.401k_plan: Maintained for U.S.-based employees; pre-tax and Roth contributions matched each pay period (with annual true-up); 9 investment alternatives plus McDonald's stock; future participant contributions capped at 20% McDonald's stock.
employee_benefit_plans.nonqualified_supplemental_plans: Unfunded; allow tax-deferred contributions and Company-provided matching that cannot be made under 401(k) due to IRS limits; investment alternatives are market-rate alternatives under the 401(k) Plan net of expenses.
employee_benefit_plans.hedging: McDonald's Corporation has entered into derivative contracts on behalf of McDonald's USA, LLC to hedge market-driven changes in certain liabilities; changes in liabilities and derivative fair value recorded in SG&A.

related_party_transactions.uncharged_admin_services_from_parent: McDonald's Corporation has provided time and expenses for certain administrative functions without charge; these costs are not reflected in the Company's results.
related_party_transactions.intercompany_payable_interest_rate: 12-month swap rate + 1% at the beginning of the period
related_party_transactions.allocated_expenses_from_parent: McDonald's incurs certain expenses (legal, share-based compensation, other SG&A) on behalf of McDonald's USA, LLC and allocates them ratably; recorded in SG&A.

subsequent_events.description: $2.4B of payable arrangements with McDonald's matured and were subsequently converted to equity. As a result, this no longer represents an obligation for the Company.
subsequent_events.subsequent_events_evaluation_date: Implied through the audit report date 2025-03-14

comprehensive_income.applicability: FASB Topic 220 does not apply since there are no items of other comprehensive income in any period presented.

cross_references_to_existing_canonical.advertising_actuals_now_quantified: Existing canonical only had the 4% Item 6 floor; notes show actual company-owned restaurant ad costs $94.9M (2024), $92.0M (2023), $84.0M (2022) plus production/marketing in SG&A $84.6M (2024).
cross_references_to_existing_canonical.royalty_to_parent_now_disclosed: Notes disclose the parent IP royalty is 2% of all company-owned and franchised restaurant sales, totaling $1,069.4M in 2024 — this was not previously surfaced in the canonical.
cross_references_to_existing_canonical.subsequent_event_now_disclosed: $2.4B intercompany payable converted to equity Feb 17 2025 — not previously surfaced.
cross_references_to_existing_canonical.tax_examination_window_now_disclosed: No examinations for years before 2009 — not previously surfaced.
cross_references_to_existing_canonical.lease_accounting_split_now_disclosed: Operating vs finance lease split, weighted-average discount rates 4.4% / 4.1%, weighted-average remaining lease term 17yrs / 28yrs — not previously surfaced.
cross_references_to_existing_canonical.future_minimum_rent_receivable_now_discl: $14.16B undiscounted future minimum rent receivable from franchisees ($1.46B in 2025) — not previously surfaced.
cross_references_to_existing_canonical.field_office_impairment_grouping_now_dis: Long-lived asset impairment is tested at the field office level, not individual restaurant — relevant policy disclosure not previously surfaced.
